port.markdownDescription:
TCP port to listen on for events from Logstash's tcp output

TCP port where Log10x listens for events sent by Logstash's `tcp` output with `codec => json_lines`. Match this against the `port` in your Logstash `output { tcp { ... } }` block. (Default: 5044)

path.markdownDescription:
Unix domain socket path to listen on (Linux/macOS)

When set, Log10x listens on a Unix domain socket instead of TCP — useful on Linux/macOS to avoid TCP overhead. Configure Logstash's `unix` output plugin with `codec => json_lines` and a matching `path`. Ignored on Windows.

messageField.markdownDescription:
Name of the JSON field carrying the original log line

Name of the field in each Logstash event that contains the actual log message text. The default (`message`) matches Logstash's convention for the `file`, `beats`, `tcp`, and `http` inputs. Set to e.g. `log` if your sources emit the log line under a different key. (Default: message)

host.markdownDescription:
TCP host of the Logstash tcp input receiving processed events

Hostname or IP where the Logstash `tcp` input is listening for processed events from Log10x. Pairs with `logstashOutputPort`. (Default: 127.0.0.1)

port.markdownDescription:
TCP port of the Logstash tcp input receiving processed events

TCP port where the Logstash `tcp` input is listening for processed events from Log10x. MUST differ from the port Log10x's own input listens on (default `5044`) or the two would collide on the same socket. (Default: 5045)

encodeType.markdownDescription:
Output format when outputFields are set. Possible values: [json, delimited]

Specifies how the combined output (main event field plus outputFields) is encoded when writing back to Logstash. Possible values: - **json**: formats all fields as a JSON object - **delimited**: formats field values separated by the output delimiter Only takes effect when logstashOutputFields is set. (Default: delimited)
